text.title: PMBOK 7 Eight Performance Domains
text.summary: All eight PMBOK 7 performance domains with purpose, outcomes, key activities, and related methodologies.

section.title: Domain Framework
section.p: Performance domains are concurrent and interconnected — all eight are active throughout the project lifecycle. No domain is sequential to another. Neglecting any one domain produces measurable degradation in others through documented interaction chains.

rule.statement: Assess all eight domains in a single sitting; partial assessments are misleading because domain health is interdependent.
rule.rationale: A green Planning domain masks a red Uncertainty domain until a risk materializes; partial snapshots create false confidence.

section.title: 1 — Stakeholder
section.p: Effectively engage all stakeholders throughout the project.

list.item: Outcomes: stakeholders identified, engagement strategies active, satisfaction achieved
Key activities: stakeholder register, engagement level assessment, communications planning
Related: stakeholder-register, stakeholder-engagement methodologies

section.title: 2 — Team
section.p: Establish a high-performing project team with shared ownership and continuous development.

list.item: Outcomes: collaborative environment, distributed leadership, continuous development
Key activities: team culture, role clarity (RACI), resource management
Related: team-development, raci-matrix methodologies

section.title: 3 — Development Approach
section.p: Select and adapt the optimal delivery approach — Predictive, Iterative, Incremental, Agile, or Hybrid.

list.item: Outcomes: approach aligned with project characteristics, continuous improvement incorporated
Key activities: approach selection, cadence definition, delivery model tailoring
Related: agile-hybrid-approaches methodology

section.title: 4 — Planning
section.p: Organize project work with realistic scope, schedule, budget, and resource plans.

list.item: Outcomes: articulated scope, achievable schedule, resource plans in place
Key activities: scope definition, WBS, schedule development, cost estimation, communications planning
Related: work-breakdown-structure, schedule-development, cost-estimation methodologies

section.title: 5 — Project Work
section.p: Establish processes and perform work efficiently with appropriate communication and resource management.

list.item: Outcomes: efficient work processes, knowledge sharing, procurements handled
Key activities: plan execution, change management, procurement management
Related: change-control, procurement-management methodologies

section.title: 6 — Delivery
section.p: Deliver value and outcomes: scope completed, quality met, stakeholders accept deliverables, benefits realized.

list.item: Outcomes: requirements delivered, quality validated, benefits realized
Key activities: quality management, scope validation, benefits realization, project closure
Related: quality-management, benefits-realization, project-closure methodologies

section.title: 7 — Measurement
section.p: Assess project performance with reliable, timely data and generate accurate forecasts.

list.item: Outcomes: reliable decision data, clear performance picture, accurate forecasts
Key activities: metrics definition, progress tracking (EVM), forecasting, lessons learned
Related: earned-value-management, lessons-learned methodologies

section.title: 8 — Uncertainty
section.p: Address uncertainty and risk: threats reduced, opportunities captured, project outcomes protected.

list.item: Outcomes: threats minimized, opportunities captured, reserves appropriate
Key activities: risk identification, analysis, response planning, monitoring
Related: risk-management methodology
